_README: Global trucking catalogue. All trucks live HERE (not in equipment.json) regardless of billing model. Hourly trucks have `hourly_rate` and `capacity_cu_yd`; per-load trucks have `per_load_rate`. Trucks have commercial insurance, so the 16% rental insurance surcharge never applies. DEFAULT: BMDW does the trucking in-house at $100/hr (catalogue key `in_house_trucking`) UNLESS Michael explicitly names a contractor (DHK / Upland's / Browns River).

in_house_trucking.name: In-house trucking ($100/hr)
in_house_trucking.notes: BMDW's own truck. $100/hr — flat hourly rate. Capacity 3 cu_yd per load (small truck). DEFAULT — use this unless Michael explicitly names a contractor.

uplands_trucking.name: Upland's Trucking
uplands_trucking.notes: $170/hr. 9 cu_yd capacity. Use when Michael says Upland's is doing the trucking.

dhk_trucking.name: DHK Trucking
dhk_trucking.notes: $150/hr. 9 cu_yd capacity. Use when Michael says DHK is doing the trucking.

tandem_dump_brownsriver.name: Tandem dump truck (Browns River)
tandem_dump_brownsriver.notes: $160/hr. Use when Michael says trucking is subbed to Browns River (Courtenay/Comox/Cumberland area).

truck_and_pup.name: Truck and pup
truck_and_pup.notes: ~16 yd^3 (tandem + pup trailer). $205/hr. Used for big aggregate hauls when Michael explicitly requests it.

block_delivery.name: Block delivery (heavy haul, per load)
block_delivery.notes: Heavy-haul truck for retaining-wall blocks. 16 lock blocks per load OR 26 magnum stones per load. Same $850 either way. Compute loads = ceil(block_count / capacity).
